title: Figma-style design canvas with object selection lock and live properties panel
business_context: Design tool: shared canvas with shapes/text/images. Object selection broadcast (so peers see what you're hovering/selecting). Properties edit live (color picker, size). Scale: 20k canvases, 10 designers per hot canvas.
constraints: Object-level lock when properties panel open
failure_modes_to_handle: Property change while another deletes object
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for canvas + objects. Selection in Redis (ephemeral). Property changes are tiny patches.\n2. Lock per object via Redis SETNX with TTL.\n3. Adapter: Redis.

database_design.schema: const CanvasSchema=new Schema({_id:String,name:String,version:{type:Number,default:0}});\nconst CanvasObjectSchema=new Schema({_id:String,canvasId:{type:String,index:true},type:String,props:Schema.Types.Mixed,z:Number,deletedAt:Date,version:{type:Number,default:1}});\nCanvasObjectSchema.index({canvasId:1,z:1});

race_conditions_addressed: Same object props edited simultaneously: version check rejects stale.
Delete + patch race: patch fails version check; UI shows 'deleted' notice.
Selection broadcast flood: server-side rate-limit middleware caps 30Hz.
Bulk select: single 'select:set' with array — one emit.
scaling_considerations: 10x: 5k objects render — virtualize viewport client-side.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: store all objects as JSON in canvas doc. Breaks: every props change rewrites 5MB doc.

title: Gaming guild chat with role-based mentions (@officers)
business_context: MMO guild chat: roles (member/officer/leader). @officers pings all officers. Role changes propagate to chat. Scale: 1k guilds × 100 members.
failure_modes_to_handle: Role demoted but still receives officer mention
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for guild members + role + chat. Mention resolves to current role members at dispatch time.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE guild_members(guild_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,role TEXT,joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),PRIMARY KEY(guild_id,user_id));\nCREATE INDEX ON guild_members(guild_id,role);\nCREATE TABLE guild_messages(id BIGSERIAL PK,guild_id BIGINT,channel TEXT,sender_id BIGINT,body TEXT,mention_role TEXT,ts TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());
database_design.queries: Mention resolve: SELECT user_id FROM members WHERE guild_id=$1 AND role=$2.

race_conditions_addressed: Role demote during dispatch: SELECT runs after message persist; uses current roles at dispatch time.
Bulk @everyone: rate-limited to officers role; @everyone requires leader role.
Kick mid-msg: server checks membership before emit.
scaling_considerations: 10x: per-mention resolve query — cache role membership in Redis 60s.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client passes role list. Trust-broken; users could @leaders.

title: Drafting/picking phase for esports with timed turns
business_context: Pre-match draft: 10 players alternate picking from champion pool with 30s timer. If timer expires, random pick. Scale: 5k drafts/day.
failure_modes_to_handle: Two players pick same champion in window
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for draft state. Server timer authoritative.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE drafts(id BIGSERIAL PK,player_order BIGINT[],pick_idx INT DEFAULT 0,picks JSONB DEFAULT '[]',bans JSONB DEFAULT '[]',pick_started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,status TEXT);\nCREATE INDEX ON drafts(status);

race_conditions_addressed: Last-ms pick: tx + lock; server timestamp authoritative.
Disconnect: auto-pick on timeout.
Duplicate champion: check inside tx.
scaling_considerations: Per-draft contention low.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client timer. Drift = unfair.

title: Estate auction with paddle number tracking and floor bidder coordination
business_context: Hybrid online + in-room auction. Floor auctioneer raises paddle for in-room bidders; online bidders bid via app. Online and floor synced. Scale: 500 in-room × 5k online per item.
constraints: Floor bid entered by auctioneer assistant
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for transactional bids. Mongo for paddle metadata + dispute artifacts.\n2. Auctioneer interface has special role; floor:bid event.

database_design.transaction_strategy: Per-bid tx + advisory lock per lot.

race_conditions_addressed: Floor + online same ms: advisory lock + SELECT FOR UPDATE serialize.
Auctioneer mistake: dispute logged in Mongo; floor rebid possible.
Hammer during in-flight bid: tx ordering — hammer after lock; if bid wins lock, it commits before hammer reads.
scaling_considerations: In-room x online sync is the value-add; per-lot fine.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: separate floor/online tracks. Breaks: missed bids by auctioneer = bidder lawsuits.

title: Live podcast room with speaker/listener mode and raise-hand queue
business_context: Audio room (Clubhouse-like). Listener can raise hand; speaker moderates queue. Scale: 1k rooms × 500 listeners.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for room state. Queue as ordered array.

database_design.schema: const AudioRoomSchema=new Schema({_id:String,title:String,speakerIds:[String],listenerCap:{type:Number,default:1000},listenerCount:{type:Number,default:0},raiseHandQueue:[{userId:String,at:Date}],status:String,recording:Boolean});

race_conditions_addressed: Cap exceeded: $expr atomic check.
Duplicate raise: $ne dedup in update predicate.
Promote race: $pull queue + $addToSet speakers atomic.
scaling_considerations: Listener count update is a hot field — Redis counter mirror.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: in-memory state. Server restart = room dies.

title: Spreadsheet cell comment threads with @mention
business_context: Sheet cell comments (Google Sheets style). Threaded replies, @mentions ping user. Scale: 50k sheets × 100 comments avg.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for comments. Anchor remap on column insert via trigger.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE sheet_comments(id BIGSERIAL PK,sheet_id BIGINT,ref TEXT,thread_root_id BIGINT,author_id BIGINT,body TEXT,resolved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());\nCREATE INDEX ON sheet_comments(sheet_id,ref) WHERE resolved=false;

race_conditions_addressed: Column insert shifts refs: PG trigger updates all comment.ref for affected cells.
Concurrent replies: PG sequence preserves order.
Mention non-member: notification dispatched anyway — recipient sees but can't access cell (perm check in their UI).
scaling_considerations: 10x: partial index keeps unresolved queries fast.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: ref stored as 'A1'. Column insert breaks all anchors.

title: Smart-watch wearable companion: filter + send to wrist
business_context: Companion app: subset of notifications sent to watch (high-priority only). Watch state synced (DND, battery). Scale: 5M paired devices.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for watch device + filter rules + delivery log.\n2. Phone is socket-connected, relays to BLE.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE watch_devices(user_id BIGINT,device_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,battery_pct INT,ble_connected BOOLEAN,dnd BOOLEAN);\nCREATE TABLE watch_filters(user_id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,types TEXT[],min_severity TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE watch_deliveries(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id BIGINT,device_id TEXT,notif_id BIGINT,delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ,read_at TIMESTAMPTZ);
database_design.queries: Filter eval before dispatch to phone socket.

race_conditions_addressed: BLE out of range: ble_connected=false skips relay.
Filter rule change mid-flight: each dispatch reads current filter.
Read sync race: PG dedup.
scaling_considerations: 10x: filter eval at scale — cache per-user filter in Redis 60s.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: relay all. Watch dies in 1 hour from constant buzzing.

title: Industrial PLC SCADA feed with command write-back and audit
business_context: Factory control: PLCs publish state at 10Hz; operators write setpoints back. All commands audited (regulatory). Scale: 500 PLCs × 1k tags each.
constraints: Command race-prevention (only one operator active per PLC)
failure_modes_to_handle: Two operators try write same tag
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG + TimescaleDB for sample stream. Audit table append-only with hash chain.\n2. Write commands gated via per-PLC advisory lock (write session lock).

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE plc_samples(plc_id BIGINT,tag TEXT,ts TIMESTAMPTZ,value DOUBLE PRECISION,PRIMARY KEY(plc_id,tag,ts));\nSELECT create_hypertable('plc_samples','ts');\nCREATE TABLE plc_commands(id BIGSERIAL PK,plc_id BIGINT,tag TEXT,target_value DOUBLE PRECISION,issued_by BIGINT,issued_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),confirmed_by BIGINT,confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,executed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,prev_hash BYTEA,row_hash BYTEA);
database_design.queries: Insert sample + insert command with chain.
database_design.transaction_strategy: Command: pg_advisory_xact_lock(plc_id) → ensure no pending → insert + chain.

race_conditions_addressed: Two-person rule: propose by A, confirm by B (different user check).
Audit chain: prev_hash linking; tampering detectable.
Concurrent proposes: per-PLC lock serializes.
scaling_considerations: 10x: per-PLC fine; audit chain forms partition by plc_id for verify.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: single-step command. Regulator: rejected, no two-person rule.

title: In-call screen control delegation (give viewer control of host's app)
business_context: Support call: host shares screen, viewer can request remote control. Host approves. Scale: 100 simul control sessions.
failure_modes_to_handle: Host loses control of own machine
architecture_reasoning: 1. WebRTC data channel for input events; Socket.IO for control session lifecycle.\n2. PG for session + audit.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE remote_sessions(id BIGSERIAL PK,host_id BIGINT,viewer_id BIGINT,call_id BIGINT,started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ,reason TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE remote_input_audit(id BIGSERIAL PK,session_id BIGINT,event_type TEXT,payload JSONB,ts TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Revoke during input: host can revoke at any time; client stops sending input on session:revoked.
Disconnect mid-control: socket disconnect auto-revokes session.
Audit: every input batched and logged separately from data channel hot path.
scaling_considerations: Audit volume high — batch + async write.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: no audit. Regulator/customer cannot review what happened.

title: Inspection checklist with photo upload progress
business_context: Building inspector fills checklist on tablet; each item can have photo. Photos upload to S3 with progress. Inspector and reviewer see live state. Scale: 50k inspections/day.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for inspection + items. S3 for photos (presigned PUT). Progress reported via socket.

database_design.schema: const InspectionSchema=new Schema({_id:String,siteId:String,status:String,items:[{_id:String,description:String,checked:Boolean,photoKey:String,note:String,reviewerComment:String}],signedAt:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Reviewer signs while inspector edits: sign requires all checked; checks read at sign time.
Photo upload fail: client retries; key idempotent.
Offline edits: client queue, replay on reconnect.
scaling_considerations: 10x: items array bounded; if huge, normalize to subcollection.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: file upload via socket. Breaks: large blob blocks event loop.

title: Multi-step subscription billing pipeline with idempotent retries
business_context: Recurring billing: charge → invoice PDF → email → tax remit. Each step idempotent. Customer sees status live. Scale: 1M customers.
constraints: Customer can see each step status
Tax remit only after charge clears
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for billing jobs (financial = ACID). Mongo for invoice content (PDF metadata).

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE billing_runs(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id BIGINT,period DATE,steps JSONB DEFAULT '{}',status TEXT,charge_id TEXT,invoice_id TEXT,created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),UNIQUE(user_id,period));
database_design.transaction_strategy: Each step tx with idempotency key (e.g., stripe charge with same idempotency_key).

race_conditions_addressed: Step retry: idempotency_key passed to Stripe + own steps JSON guard.
Multi-period race: UNIQUE (user, period) prevents double-run.
Tax before charge clears: linear step order enforced.
Cascade: compensation jobs queue from step failures (omitted for brevity).
scaling_considerations: 10x: BullMQ scales; PG billing_runs partition by period.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: linear async chain. Crash mid = double-charge.

title: Logistics tracking dashboard with route deviation alerts
business_context: Trucks tracked vs. planned route; deviation > 500m triggers alert. Dashboard shows live + planned. Scale: 5k trucks.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo time-series for telemetry. Geo functions for distance to route.\n2. Adapter: Redis.

database_design.schema: const TruckTelSchema=new Schema({truckId:String,ts:Date,lat:Number,lng:Number,accuracy:Number},{timeseries:{timeField:'ts',metaField:'truckId',granularity:'seconds'}});\nconst RouteSchema=new Schema({_id:String,truckId:String,waypoints:[{lat:Number,lng:Number}],activeFrom:Date,activeTo:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Bad GPS: accuracy filter.
Route changed mid-route: each ingest reads current active route.
Storm: dedup deviation per truck within 60s window (client-side easy).
scaling_considerations: 10x: distanceToPolyline O(segments) per ingest — precompute route segments to spatial index.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client computes deviation. Bad GPS = false alarms.

title: Tournament bracket with live match results and seeding
business_context: Esports tournament: 128-team bracket; matches complete → winner advances. Live bracket UI updates. Scale: 50 simul tournaments.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for bracket structure + matches. Mongo for match metadata (flexible: stats per game type).

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE tournaments(id BIGSERIAL PK,name TEXT,status TEXT,format TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE bracket_slots(tournament_id BIGINT,slot_id INT,team_id BIGINT,from_match_id BIGINT,PRIMARY KEY(tournament_id,slot_id));\nCREATE TABLE matches(id BIGSERIAL PK,tournament_id BIGINT,round INT,slot_a INT,slot_b INT,winner_slot INT,advances_to_slot INT,result JSONB,played_at TIMESTAMPTZ);
database_design.queries: Submit result: tx update match + advance team to next slot.
database_design.transaction_strategy: Result tx: lock match → set winner → update next slot's team_id.

race_conditions_addressed: Double submit: WHERE winner_slot IS NULL guard.
Cycle: schema cannot represent (slot_id PK + advances_to_slot one-way).
Reseed: separate admin op tx.
scaling_considerations: 10x: per-tournament fine.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: separate match table + ad-hoc advance. Breaks: race or admin error advances wrong team.

title: Chat with end-of-conversation summary by LLM
business_context: Support chat: when closed, LLM summarizes conversation for ticket record. Customer + agent see same summary. Scale: 100k chats/day.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for chats. Mongo for summaries (LLM output text + edits).

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE support_chats(id BIGSERIAL PK,status TEXT,closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nconst SummarySchema=new Schema({_id:String,chatId:String,text:String,editedBy:String,editedAt:Date,llmTokens:Number,llmCostCents:Number});
database_design.queries: On close: enqueue summary; persist on done.

race_conditions_addressed: Close before persist: LLM input fetched after close commits.
Edit during stream: edit overrides; final stream commit ignored if version newer.
API error: ack returns error; agent retries.
scaling_considerations: LLM cost: throttle close-rate; batch quiet hours.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: bulk LLM call at end of day. Agent has to wait for summary.

title: Open-office desk-booking + 'who's at the office today'
business_context: Hybrid work: book desk; office floor plan shows occupants. See who's in today. Scale: 10k employees × 5 offices.
constraints: One desk per person per day
Check-out auto at end of day
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for bookings + desk plan.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE desks(id BIGSERIAL PK,office_id BIGINT,x INT,y INT);\nCREATE TABLE desk_bookings(desk_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,booked_date DATE,checked_in_at TIMESTAMPTZ,checked_out_at TIMESTAMPTZ,visible BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,PRIMARY KEY(desk_id,booked_date));\nCREATE INDEX ON desk_bookings(user_id,booked_date);
database_design.queries: Book: INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; per-user check: SELECT EXISTS.

race_conditions_addressed: Double book: PK (desk_id,date) + per-user EXISTS check.
Privacy: server-side filter on emit (visible flag).
Auto-checkout: nightly job sets checked_out_at.
scaling_considerations: Per-office room fine.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client trust user_id. Privacy leaked.

title: Music score editor with collaborative markings (fingering, dynamics)
business_context: Conductor edits orchestral score; musicians add fingerings/dynamics live. Scale: 500 scores × 50 musicians.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for markings (varied shape: fingering/dynamic/articulation). PG for score metadata + parts.
race_conditions_addressed: Concurrent on same measure: each is a separate Marking doc.
Part reassignment: server check on add.
Delete by author only: WHERE authorId guard.
scaling_considerations: Indexes keep per-part fetch fast.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: store markings as JSON array on score. Breaks: 50 musicians × 1000 measures = giant doc.

title: GDPR-compliant notification with right-to-erasure cascade
business_context: EU customer notifications must be erasable on request. Notifications + dispatch logs + audit must be purged within 30 days. Scale: 5M EU users.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG with per-user FK; erasure deletes all per-user rows + flags audit row as anonymized.\n2. BullMQ for delayed cascade.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE erasure_requests(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id BIGINT,requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending');\nCREATE TABLE erasure_audit(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id_hash TEXT,action TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());
database_design.queries: Cascade DELETE across notification tables WHERE user_id=$1.
database_design.transaction_strategy: Cascade tx wrapping all user-data DELETEs.

race_conditions_addressed: In-flight notification: tx ensures all-or-nothing.
Partial fail: tx rollback; retry via BullMQ.
Audit anonymization: hash stored, not raw user_id.
scaling_considerations: 10x: cascade is bulk DELETE — partition tables by user_id range; chunked DELETE.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: hard delete with no audit. Regulator: no proof of compliance.

title: Indoor air quality (IAQ) network with per-room mood overlay
business_context: Open offices: per-room CO2/PM2.5 sensors at 0.1Hz. Slack-style status mapping (green/yellow/red). Scale: 5k rooms.
failure_modes_to_handle: Subscribe storm at start of day
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo time-series for samples + materialized current-state collection.

database_design.schema: const IaqSampleSchema=new Schema({roomId:String,ts:Date,co2:Number,pm25:Number,temp:Number,humidity:Number},{timeseries:{timeField:'ts',metaField:'roomId',granularity:'minutes'}});\nconst RoomStateSchema=new Schema({_id:String,roomId:String,mood:String,co2:Number,pm25:Number,updatedAt:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Flap: only alert on red entry from non-red.
Cleaning chemical spike: report but mood logic handles.
scaling_considerations: 10x: time-series compresses well.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: emit every sample to all. Bandwidth waste.

title: WebRTC fallback to MJPEG over WebSocket for low-bandwidth networks
business_context: Healthcare patient video: WebRTC preferred, but if bandwidth < threshold or NAT traversal fails, fall back to MJPEG-over-WebSocket. Scale: 500 simul calls.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for call state. PG for fallback usage metrics.

database_design.schema: const RtcCallSchema=new Schema({_id:String,patientId:String,doctorId:String,mode:String,fallbackReason:String,startedAt:Date,endedAt:Date});\nCREATE TABLE fallback_metrics(call_id TEXT,from_mode TEXT,to_mode TEXT,reason TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Concurrent fallback reports: both update mode='mjpeg'; idempotent.
Network change: client re-evaluates and emits new mode if differs.
Reconnect: call state has current mode.
scaling_considerations: MJPEG much higher bandwidth — limit to truly degraded paths.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: WebRTC only. NAT-strict networks = no call.

title: Survey designer with live preview to co-author and respondent test mode
business_context: Survey-design tool: 2 authors collaborate on questions; live preview side-panel; can test as respondent. Scale: 30k surveys.
failure_modes_to_handle: Logic cycle (Q5 visible iff Q5 answered)
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for survey + questions + logic.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE surveys(id BIGSERIAL PK,title TEXT,status TEXT,version INT DEFAULT 0);\nCREATE TABLE questions(id BIGSERIAL PK,survey_id BIGINT,position INT,type TEXT,text TEXT,options JSONB,visible_if JSONB);\nCREATE INDEX ON questions(survey_id,position);

race_conditions_addressed: Reorder race: bulk tx; second reorder runs after first commits, applies its own order.
Publish race: WHERE status='draft' guard.
Logic cycle: detect via topo walk in publish handler (omitted).
scaling_considerations: Per-survey low contention.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client patches without server validation. Publish-broken surveys.

title: AI-assisted email triage with confidence-gated auto-categorization
business_context: Inbox AI: incoming email classified; if confidence > 0.9 auto-route, else queue for human review. Scale: 100k email/day.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for emails + classifications. BullMQ for triage worker.

database_design.schema: const EmailSchema=new Schema({_id:String,from:String,subject:String,body:String,category:String,confidence:Number,status:String,assignedReviewer:String,classifiedAt:Date,reviewedAt:Date,actualCategory:String});

race_conditions_addressed: Two claim same: assignedReviewer null guard.
Override after auto-route: reviewer view shows all emails, override creates training feedback record.
API quota: throttle worker concurrency.
scaling_considerations: Worker pool per concurrent classification budget.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: instant route all. Bad categorization = customer never replies.

title: Calendar with shared edit and conflict-resolution UI
business_context: Team calendar: 5 people edit events. Time conflicts highlighted. Scale: 100k calendars.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for events + recurring rules + attendees.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE cal_events(id BIGSERIAL PK,calendar_id BIGINT,title TEXT,starts_at TIMESTAMPTZ,ends_at TIMESTAMPTZ,rrule TEXT,exdates TIMESTAMPTZ[],version INT DEFAULT 1);\nCREATE TABLE cal_attendees(event_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,status TEXT,PRIMARY KEY(event_id,user_id));\nCREATE INDEX ON cal_events(calendar_id,starts_at);

race_conditions_addressed: Reschedule conflict: version check fails second update.
Concurrent respond: UPSERT idempotent.
scaling_considerations: 10x: composite index keeps range queries fast.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: rrule expansion client-side. Different machines compute different instances.

title: Fraud detection live feed with rule-engine override
business_context: Payments fraud team: transactions flagged by ML/rules; analyst reviews and decides. Scale: 100k txn/min, 50 analysts.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for transactions + flags + decisions.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE flagged_txn(id BIGSERIAL PK,txn_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,amount_cents BIGINT,score NUMERIC,reason TEXT,flagged_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),assigned_analyst BIGINT,decision TEXT,decision_at TIMESTAMPTZ,sla_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nCREATE INDEX ON flagged_txn(decision,sla_at) WHERE decision IS NULL;
database_design.queries: Claim: UPDATE SET assigned_analyst WHERE assigned_analyst IS NULL.

race_conditions_addressed: Double claim: UPDATE WHERE NULL guard.
SLA: watcher emits warning, escalates to supervisor on miss.
Decision tampering: requires assigned_analyst match.
scaling_considerations: 100k/min ingest = 1.6k/sec. Partition flagged_txn by month.
observability: Metrics: sla_breach_rate, claim_p99, decision_p99 by queue.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: round-robin to analysts. No load-balance, no SLA awareness.

title: Live shopping with concurrent viewers commenting and host highlight
business_context: Live shopping stream: host showcases products; viewers comment + buy. Comments fly, host pins one. Scale: 50k viewers per stream.
constraints: Buy action linked to comment context
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for stream + purchase. Mongo for comments (high write).

database_design.schema: const StreamCommentSchema=new Schema({streamId:String,viewerId:String,body:String,pinnedAt:Date,createdAt:{type:Date,default:Date.now}});\nStreamCommentSchema.index({streamId:1,createdAt:1});\nCREATE TABLE stream_purchases(id BIGSERIAL PK,stream_id BIGINT,viewer_id BIGINT,product_id BIGINT,from_comment_id TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Burst: rate-limit per user per stream.
Spam: server-side filter.
Pin race: latest pin wins.
scaling_considerations: 10x: shard streams across nodes; Redis adapter handles fan-out.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: emit all to all. 50k viewers × 100 comments/min = 5M emits/min, bus dies.

title: Capstone: MMO server with zone handoff and seamless world
business_context: Open-world MMO: 50k players in seamless world. Server-per-zone with handoff when player crosses zone boundary. Scale: 100 zones × 500 players.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for player state (durable position + inventory). PG for zone capacity + handoff logs.\n2. Each zone is one Node process. Handoff: source zone marks player 'handoff_pending', dest zone accepts, then source removes.

database_design.queries: Handoff: tx update PlayerState + zone counters.
database_design.transaction_strategy: Two-phase: source marks pending → dest accepts and clears pending.

socket_io_design.adapter_choice: redis (only for cross-zone chat; gameplay is single-node per zone).

race_conditions_addressed: Boundary oscillation: handoff lock prevents re-handoff while pending.
Dest full: capacity UPDATE conditional; rollback handoff state.
Crash during handoff: state has handoffPending; recovery resolves: if dest accepted via handoffs table, complete; else clear pending.
Double-spawn: source emits 'leave' only after dest emits 'complete' broadcast back.
Inventory transfer: PlayerState single doc — atomic with zoneId change.
scaling_considerations: 10x (500 zones, 250k players): per-zone sharding by hash; central handoff coordinator vs per-pair; cross-zone chat via Redis pub/sub.
observability: Metrics: handoff_p99, handoff_fail_rate, zone_capacity_pct, crash_recovery_count. Logs: every handoff with both zone IDs. Alerts: handoff_fail > 1%, capacity near 100% per zone.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: single big server. Breaks: 50k players in one event loop = unplayable. Per-zone sticky routing solves it but introduces handoff complexity — without proper two-phase tx, players duplicate or disappear. Killer bug: server crash mid-handoff leaves player in both zones (visible to both) or neither — items can be duplicated by triggering crash mid-trade across zone boundary.
